comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the Baochip-1x project, with users expressing excitement, asking questions, and sharing insights. Several comments highlight the contributions of the project's creator, Bunnie, while others raise concerns about transparency and the technical aspects of the chip.

comments.author: bunnie
comments.text: Hello wonderful people! I'm bunnie - just noticed this is on HN. Unfortunately due to timezones I'm about to afk for a bit.
comments.analysis: Bunnie's comment is direct and engages the community, indicating a willingness to participate in the discussion.

comments.author: luma
comments.text: bunnie your book "Hacking the XBox" taught me how to get started on reversing electronics, took the fear out of the process...
comments.analysis: This comment expresses gratitude and personal impact, adding a positive tone to the discussion.

comments.author: vintagedave
comments.text: This is wonderful! Also what a fantastic partnership that allowed adding a new CPU to that die. Kudos to them.
comments.analysis: The comment is supportive and acknowledges the collaborative effort behind the project, though it raises a valid concern about licensing.

comments.author: genxy
comments.text: To anyone from crowdsupply listening, please turn down your VPN check. I am not stripping my privacy protection to use your site.
comments.analysis: This comment highlights a user experience issue, which is important for accessibility but lacks broader context.

comments.author: chuckadams
comments.text: > What’s a banker going to do with the source code of a chip, anyway? Hand it to someone who does know what to do with it.
comments.analysis: The comment provides a logical perspective on the importance of source code availability, though it could be seen as dismissive.

comments.author: bArray
comments.text: > Those with a bit of silicon savvy would note that it’s not cheap to produce such a chip, yet, I have not raised a dollar of venture capital.
comments.analysis: This comment raises important questions about the project's funding and logistics but lacks depth in exploration.

comments.author: alexisread
comments.text: Great work on the chip, I’m really onboard with the trusted computing aim! Is there a way to bootstrap binary code into the reram?
comments.analysis: The comment is supportive and poses a technical question, though it lacks context about the project's specifics.

comments.author: Dani99
comments.analysis: This comment is irrelevant to the discussion and does not contribute meaningfully.

comments.author: vintagedave
comments.text: This is wonderful! Also what a fantastic partnership that allowed adding a new CPU to that die. Kudos to them.
comments.analysis: While positive, this comment lacks depth and does not engage with the technical aspects of the project.

comments.author: the_biot
comments.text: Why the few closed-source components on the system? You mention the bus, USB PHY etc -- are those things harder to design than the CPU core?
comments.analysis: This comment raises a pertinent question regarding the design choices but could benefit from more context.

comments.analysis: This comment expresses gratitude and personal impact, adding a positive tone to the discussion.

comments.author: awesomeusername
comments.text: I run a hardware company now (thankfully in the age of AI), as a direct consequence of reading Bunnies book 'hardware hacker'...
comments.analysis: This comment shares a personal success story, contributing positively to the discussion.
